title: Rename active executable identity from herdctl to herdr-go
action: Apply the locked full identity rename and fix the goal-check miss: Cargo package/bin becomes herdr-go, Rust lib crate/imports become herdr_go, public env vars become HERDR_GO_*, token/state files become herdr-go.env and herdr-go-state.sqlite, installer/release/CI/smoke/service/docs/tests use herdr-go, current source fixtures use herdr-go when they name this product, and no herdctl compatibility alias/fallback/shim remains. Preserve historical docs/history evidence unless it is a current-facing spec or learning that actively instructs future work.

truths: The only active executable name is herdr-go (herdr-go.exe on Windows)
Cargo package/bin/default-run are herdr-go and the library crate/import namespace is herdr_go
Runtime env vars use HERDR_GO_* only, with no HERDCTL_* fallback
Token and sqlite state filenames are herdr-go.env and herdr-go-state.sqlite
Installer, dev deploy, service templates, release workflow, CI, and Windows smoke all reference the renamed executable consistently
Current README/specs/tests do not present herdctl as active; historical evidence remains untouched

artifacts.substantive: HERDR_GO_* env vars and herdr-go.env token file

key_links: Release producer and installer consumer must agree on the executable filename
Doctor/config/main must agree on HERDR_GO_WEB_SECRET and herdr-go.env
prohibitions: Do not add a herdctl alias, symlink, fallback env var, or migration path
Do not rewrite docs/history audit records just to remove old wording
Do not change Herdr upstream binary naming or protocol behavior
Do not publish or tag a release

verify: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Replaced the remaining retired product-name fixture strings in the web module with herdr-go.
